text.title: typescript-strict-mode Worked Example
text.summary: Migrating faion-net-be from strict=false to the 2026 baseline.

list.item: tsc baseline: ~340 strict errors, ~210 noUncheckedIndexedAccess errors, ~70 exactOptional errors

example.code: { "artefact_id": "billing-strict-migration", "owner": "ruslan@faion.net", "repo": "faion-net-be", "ts_version": "^5.4.0", "current_flags": {"strict": false}, "target_flags": {"strict": true, "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true, "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true}, "migration_steps": [ {"order": 1, "flag": "strict", "fix_strategy": "Enable bundle; address strictNullChecks errors by adding | undefined to optional returns and adding explicit return types.", "error_budget": 80}, {"order": 2, "flag": "noUncheckedIndexedAccess", "fix_strategy": "Destructure with default OR optional chain at every array[i] / record[key] read site. Use lodash-style at-helpers if a default is common.", "error_budget": 80}, {"order": 3, "flag": "exactOptionalPropertyTypes", "fix_strategy": "Audit optional fields; add | undefined where explicit undefined assignment is part of the contract (e.g., reset states).", "error_budget": 30} ], "lint_backstops": ["@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion", "@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any", "@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment", "@typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment"], "version": "1.0.0", "last_reviewed": "2026-05-22" }
